reasoning: The macro signals are strongly aligned. The US Treasury yield curve is upward sloping (normal), which signifies market expectations of future economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4]. This is supported by the 10-year real interest rate being low relative to history (0.16%, 14th percentile), which is associated with increased borrowing, higher liquidity, and expansionary GDP [2328dabf508c, 99ad6cdde172]. Furthermore, real rates near zero typically lead to higher PE valuation multiples for equity indices [5e068f4bde46]. The low VIX (13.9) is consistent with the suppressed volatility expected when real rates are very low [0826c79434b2, 99ad6cdde172]. While the yield curve trend is falling, it remains firmly in the 'normal' regime, maintaining the bullish outlook.
